source: USDA FoodData Central — approximate per-serving values

chicken breast.note: Lean protein king. Skin-on adds ~70 cal and 8g fat per serving.

chicken thigh.healthier_swap: Remove skin to save ~50 cal and 6g fat
chicken thigh.note: More flavorful than breast due to higher fat content.

ground beef 80/20.healthier_swap: Use 93/7 lean ground (170 cal, 24g protein, 8g fat) or ground turkey
ground beef 80/20.note: 80/20 is best for burgers (juicy). 93/7 can be dry — add a little olive oil.

salmon.note: High in omega-3. The fat is the good kind — don't avoid it.

white rice.healthier_swap: Cauliflower rice (25 cal/cup) or brown rice (215 cal, 5g fiber)
white rice.note: High glycemic. If cutting carbs, halve the portion and bulk up with veggies.

brown rice.note: Only slightly fewer calories than white, but way more fiber and nutrients.

pasta.healthier_swap: Legume pasta (190 cal, 14g protein, 11g fiber) or zucchini noodles (30 cal)
pasta.note: Portion is key — restaurant portions are usually 2-3x a single serving.

olive oil.healthier_swap: Avocado oil spray for cooking (fewer calories per coverage)
olive oil.note: Healthy fat. Don't fear it, but measure it — it adds up fast.

butter.healthier_swap: For cooking: olive oil or avocado oil. For baking: keep butter (it matters for texture).
butter.note: In baking, butter is irreplaceable for flaky texture and flavor.

egg.healthier_swap: Egg whites only (17 cal, 4g protein, 0 fat) — but you lose the nutrients in the yolk
egg.note: The yolk has the vitamins. Whole eggs are worth it unless you're strictly cutting calories.

avocado.note: High calorie for a fruit, but packed with fiber, potassium, and healthy monounsaturated fat.

greek yogurt.note: Excellent mayo/sour cream substitute. Full-fat version is creamier (190 cal).

mayonnaise.healthier_swap: Greek yogurt + whole grain mustard (25 cal/tbsp, 3g protein, creamy, tangy)
mayonnaise.note: Mostly fat. The Greek yogurt swap works great in chicken salad, pasta salad, dressings.

sweet potato.note: Lower glycemic than white potatoes, more fiber, more vitamin A. Great complex carb.

potato.healthier_swap: Sweet potato for lower glycemic index, or cauliflower mash (40 cal/cup)
potato.note: It's what you PUT on the potato that matters. Butter + sour cream can double the calories.

broccoli.note: One of the most nutrient-dense foods. Don't overcook — steamed or roasted until bright green.

estimate.note: Almost zero calorie. It's the dressing that counts.
Calorie-dense but satiating. Measure them — a 'handful' is often 2-3 servings.

estimate.healthier_swap: Nutritional yeast (20 cal/tbsp, cheesy flavor) or reduced-fat versions
estimate.note: Hard cheeses pack more flavor per gram — use less for the same impact.

mayo → greek_yogurt_mustard.best_for: Chicken salad, pasta salad, dressings, dips

pasta → legume_pasta.best_for: Any pasta dish — texture is slightly different but close

sugar → reduce_25_percent.gains: Less sweetness, often more balanced flavor
sugar → reduce_25_percent.best_for: Most baked goods — recipes usually over-sweeten
